title: Adjustable Router Table Fence
summary: A plywood router table fence with slotted support walls, so the face plates slide to fit different bit widths.
description:
A fixed router-table fence only works for the one bit-to-fence distance it was built for; this fence's support walls are slotted instead of solid, which lets the whole face plate assembly slide and lock at whatever gap the current bit needs.

Built as a companion to the mobile shop router table elsewhere in this catalog, but it works on any router table with t-track mounting holes at the right spacing.

A **horizontal t-track above the face plates** is what turns this from a plain fence into a mounting point for bit guards, stops, and featherboards — without it, every accessory needs its own separate clamping solution.

tools.note: Routs the adjustment channel in the support walls.

steps.title: Cut the base
steps.body: Cut the fence base from 3/4" plywood and drill mounting holes sized to your t-track bolts.

steps.title: Cut and slot the support walls
steps.body: Cut the two support walls and rout a 3/8in adjustment channel in each so the face plates can slide.

steps.title: Assemble the sliding fence
steps.body: Assemble the support walls to the base with carriage bolts, washers, and wing nuts so the face plates can slide and lock at any position.

steps.title: Install the t-track
steps.body: Install the t-track sections, including a horizontal track above the face plates for mounting bit guards, stops, and featherboards.

steps.title: Attach the face plates
steps.body: Attach the adjustable face plates to the support walls with knobs and t-track bolts.
